verdict_reasoning: ABCB9 (TAPL) is a half-transporter of the ABC family that resides in the lysosomal membrane, where it transports cytosolic peptides into the lysosomal lumen. It is an endomembrane-resident multipass TM protein with no documented stable plasma-membrane pool. Checking contextual buckets: (a) no documented cancer/cell-death/activation-induced ecto-display of ABCB9; (b) no narrow germline/developmental surface-restricted display (broadly expressed, testis-enriched but lysosomal there too); (c) although lysosomal, no documented lysosomal-exocytosis surfacing of ABCB9 specifically; (d) no documented PM minority pool or cycling; (e) not a soluble protein with TM-partner covalent tethering. Verdict: endomembrane-resident.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Possible undocumented surfacing via lysosomal exocytosis in secretory lysosome-containing cells (e.g., immune cells); no gene-specific evidence found.
